title: Tuning Language Models by Mixture-of-Depths Ensemble
abstract: arXiv:2410.13077v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) traditionally rely on final-layer loss for finetuning and final-layer representations for predictions, potentially overlooking the predictive power embedded in late layers. Interpretability tools such as the logit lens show that late-layer representations already carry largely formed, task-relevant predictions; here we ask whether that observation can be turned into an actionable training signal. We find that focusing tuning effort on these layers can yield losses comparable to those of the final layer, with complementary test-time behaviour. Building on this, we introduce a tuning framework, Mixture-of-Depths Ensemble (MoDE), which treats the late layers as an ensemble that contributes to the final logits through learned routing weights. MoDE can be applied on top of any existing tuning method (e.g., LoRA) and, in our experiments, modestly improves reasoning performance at a small

Title: Tuning Language Models by Mixture-of-Depths Ensemble
Abstract: arXiv:2410.13077v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) traditionally rely on final-layer loss for finetuning and final-layer representations for predictions, potentially overlooking the predictive power embedded in late layers. MoDE can be applied on top of any existing tuning method (e.g., LoRA) and, in our experiments, modestly improves reasoning performance at a small
translated_title: 基于深度混合集成的语言模型微调方法
translated_abstract: arXiv:2410.13077v2 公告类型：替换交叉 摘要：基于Transformer的大型语言模型（LLMs）传统上依赖最终层的损失进行微调，并使用最终层的表示进行预测，这可能忽略了深层中蕴含的预测能力。诸如logit透镜等可解释性工具表明，深层表示已经携带了高度成型且与任务相关的预测；本文探讨是否可以将这一观察转化为可操作的训练信号。我们发现，将微调重点放在这些层上，可以产生与最终层相当的损失，并且具有互补的测试时行为。基于此，我们引入了一个微调框架——深度混合集成（MoDE），该框架将深层视为一个集成，通过学习的路由权重对最终logits做出贡献。MoDE可以应用于任何现有微调方法（例如LoRA）之上，在我们的实验中，以较小的代价适度提升了推理性能。
tldr: 本文提出MoDE框架，将语言模型的深层视为集成，通过学习路由权重优化微调，以提升推理性能。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces the MoDE framework, which treats late layers of language models as an ensemble and optimizes fine-tuning via learned routing weights to improve reasoning performance.
